criterion_scores.rationale: 7 years of backend engineering with clear senior-level progression demonstrated by leading a team of 4 engineers and owning a critical real-time trade execution system.
Built and maintained a trade execution engine handling 50k requests per second, demonstrating hands-on experience with high-frequency, low-latency production systems.
Proven production experience building a trade execution engine at a fintech startup, directly addressing core trading infrastructure requirements.
Demonstrated experience with Kafka message broker in production trade execution system, indicating strong event-driven architecture knowledge, though WebSocket experience not explicitly mentioned.
Proficient in both Python and Go with substantial production systems built and maintained in both languages at trading infrastructure scale.
Demonstrated production experience with PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka, but Docker and Kubernetes experience not explicitly mentioned.
6 years of backend engineering at prestigious institutions (Goldman Sachs, Barclays) indicates senior-level experience, though specific team leadership or mentoring not mentioned.
Explicit low-latency focus in equity derivatives pricing systems at tier-1 financial institutions suggests deep experience with performance-critical infrastructure.
6 years working on equity derivatives pricing and trading systems at Goldman Sachs and Barclays demonstrates strong production fintech experience in regulated environments.
No explicit mention of WebSocket protocols, message brokers, or event-driven architecture experience, which are core requirements for this criterion.
Java listed as primary language with only some Python experience, not meeting the requirement for demonstrated strong proficiency in Python or Go.
No mention of PostgreSQL, NoSQL databases, Docker, or Kubernetes experience in the candidate summary.
4 years of backend engineering experience falls short of the 5+ years expected for senior-level roles, with no mention of team leadership or critical system ownership.
No evidence of building or optimizing systems handling >10k requests per second or documented performance optimizations mentioned.
Contributed to trading libraries and built WebSocket pipeline for crypto exchange, showing domain familiarity but lacking production trading system ownership.
Built a WebSocket data pipeline for crypto exchange demonstrating practical experience with real-time data protocols and event-driven systems.
Noted as Python expert with substantial contributions to multiple trading libraries, demonstrating deep language expertise and production experience.

5 years of backend engineering meets the threshold for senior-level roles; strong communication noted by referees suggests leadership potential but specific team ownership not mentioned.
100k transactions per day translates to roughly 1 transaction per second, well below the >10k requests per second threshold for high-throughput systems experience.
